# Repository Agent Evaluation Studies (2026)

## Overview

Research signals from 2026 showing a shift from generic prompt/loop discourse toward measurable repository-agent operations.

## Key Research Directions

### 1. AGENTS.md Efficiency
- AGENTS.md files can reduce runtime and output-token use while preserving comparable task behavior
- Repository-level instruction files as a new form of agent configuration

### 2. Repository-Leveraging Benchmarks
- Emphasis on realistic task contracts, execution outputs, and practical acceptance criteria
- Moving beyond toy tasks to real-world repository operations

### 3. Agentic Optimization
- Beyond binary correctness toward multi-objective codebase-level performance
- Tradeoffs: speed, quality, token usage, code review acceptance

### 4. Skill Security
- From isolated SKILL.md scanning toward repository-context classification
- Abandoned-repository risk analysis (see repository-context-security skill)

### 5. Soft-Verified Repository-Agent Training
- Generated trajectories for training
- Repository-specific specialization
- Claude Code integration as research artifact

## Target Agent Runtimes
- Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, OpenHands, SWE-Agent, Aider

## AOMK Value
- Provides evaluation frameworks for skill quality assessment
- Informs skill authoring standards for repository-context agents
- Grounds AOMK distillation in measurable performance criteria

## Related Skills
- repository-context-security: supply chain security analysis
- agent-skill-portability: cross-runtime skill compatibility
- skillops: automated skill library maintenance
